title: Europe by Rail — July 2026 (Milan · Amalfi · Bernina · Paris · Barcelona · Madrid)

legs.note: Book a morning flight so Como is doable the same afternoon.
Arrival-afternoon day trip to Lake Como.
Day trip. NOTE: dropping this gives Amalfi a needed extra day — see plan caveats.

By train (preference). Frecciarossa/Italo — but summer-2026 works inflate it to ~7h (not the usual ~4h07). Earliest Italo 07:50→15:03 is the only departure that nearly catches the last Positano ferry (15:35); else car transfer or overnight Naples. Book trenitalia.com / italotreno.com.
Aim for the latest catchable sailing; if the 7h train lands ~15:03, the 15:35 ferry is a sprint — fallback is a car transfer. Operator sites (NLG, Positano.com).
EARLY ferry — Jul 8 is the repositioning day to reach Tirano before the booked Bernina. Amalfi = 2 nights (Jul 6, 7), depart Jul 8 AM.
MUST fly, not train — the same-day hop to Tirano for the booked Bernina is impossible with the ~7h train. Naples→Milan ~1h20.

Trenord direct regionale ~2h20 (trenord.it). Arrive Tirano evening Jul 8 → overnight, ready for the 08:06 Bernina. Marked manual: Italian domestic regional isn't in the open APIs (they misroute via Switzerland).
BOOKED — Bernina Express, Tirano 08:06 → Chur 12:31, Jul 9. Panoramic, seat reservation held (rhb.ch).
Onward to the Jungfrau region right off the Bernina (~3h via Zürich/Bern + Interlaken Ost). Camp in Lauterbrunnen valley.
Jungfrau → Paris via Interlaken Ost → Bern → TGV Lyria. ~6h, full travel day. Two camping nights (Jul 9, 10) in Lauterbrunnen first.
AVE ~2.5–3h, frequent. Renfe has no public API — book on renfe.com / Trainline.
Fly home. Trip now ends Jul 17 (a day earlier) because the Bernina booking pulled the back half forward.
